scope: Advances in the development and use of computational methods for the solution of problems in engineering and the sciences. Establishing appropriate mathematical models and their numerical solution in all areas of mechanics, including solids, structures, fluids, and multiphysics. The word 'structures' is interpreted broadly to include solids, fluids, and multiphysics. Focus on automatic solution of mathematical models, multiphysics and multiscale phenomena, uncertainty quantification, optimization algorithms, and complete lifecycle simulation. Application domains include civil and environmental engineering, mechanical engineering, biomechanics, automotive, aeronautical, and ocean engineering.

manuscript_structure.abstract_note: Concise and factual. State purpose, methods, principal results, and major conclusions. Avoid references and non-standard abbreviations.
manuscript_structure.keywords_note: Written in English. Avoid multi-word keywords with and/of. Only established abbreviations.
manuscript_structure.highlights_note: 3-5 bullet points, max 85 characters each including spaces. Capture novel techniques and key insights.
manuscript_structure.graphical_abstract_note: Optional. Summarize contents in concise pictorial form.
manuscript_structure.novelty_note: Papers must present novel and general techniques or offer substantial new insights into important methods. Novel techniques require comparison with established advanced methods.

formatting.your_paper_your_way: Flexible formatting for initial submission; strict formatting at revision stage

references.format: Numbered in square brackets [1] in order of appearance
examples: [1] K.J. Bathe, E.L. Wilson, Numerical Methods in Finite Element Analysis, Prentice-Hall, Englewood Cliffs, NJ, 1976.
[2] T. Belytschko, W.K. Liu, B. Moran, K.I. Elkhodary, Nonlinear Finite Elements for Continua and Structures, second ed., Wiley, Chichester, 2014.
[3] O.C. Zienkiewicz, R.L. Taylor, J.Z. Zhu, The Finite Element Method: Its Basis and Fundamentals, seventh ed., Butterworth-Heinemann, Oxford, 2013.
references.formatting_rules: Author initials then surname. Article titles in regular text, sentence case. Journal names in italics and abbreviated (ISO). Format: Journal Volume (Year) Pages. DOI highly encouraged. More than 6 authors: list first 6 then et al.

figures_tables.placement: Each figure as separate file or end of manuscript
figures_tables.color_note: Free online; check for print color. Use RGB encoding.

submission_notes.equations: Displayed separately, numbered consecutively in parentheses
submission_notes.math_notation: MathType or Word equation editor, or LaTeX math environments. Italic for scalar variables, bold for vectors/matrices.
submission_notes.ethical_requirements: Original unpublished work, not under review elsewhere. Preprint posting on SSRN allowed.
submission_notes.ai_policy: AI tools may support preparation but must be declared. Statement placed before references.
